q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is logger.warning, which is not network I/O, shell execution, DB write, or RCE

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Return value is {'status': 'queued'}, which does not reveal whether log injection occurred in logger.warning

mock_guidance.target: Mock or spy on the logger.warning call at line 33
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the arguments passed to logger.warning, specifically the wire_text argument and the authorization header value, so that injected newline or control characters can be detected
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Allow the call to proceed without actually writing to any log output; simply record the arguments for assertion
notes: The vulnerability is that wire_text from request.body() is logged unsanitized, allowing newline injection
Assert that the captured wire_text argument does not contain \r or \n characters, or verify that it was sanitized before reaching the logger
